similarity_name: Mixed clinical covariate or patient similarity
definition: Represent patients or encounters using clinically meaningful covariates such as demographics, labs, diagnoses, medications, and utilization features. Compute mixed feature similarity, regularized Mahalanobis similarity, or a clinically weighted patient-similarity score to audit whether predictions degrade for patients unlike those in the training set.
example_usage: python tabular_mahalanobis_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --feature-cols-prefix cov_ --regularization 1e-4

citations.title: Measurement and application of patient similarity in personalized predictive modeling based on electronic medical records

evidence.summary: The paper motivates individualized prediction using similar patients from EHR features.
It compares common patient similarity measures and shows similar-patient cohorts improve prediction with fewer samples.
Patient similarity is computed by combining feature-level similarities for labs, age, sex, and diagnoses.

limitations: Clinical covariate similarity can encode protected attributes, access patterns, or site artifacts.
Mahalanobis distance assumes numeric, scaled features and can be unstable with sparse or high-dimensional data.
Clinical similarity is not causal transportability.
quality_gates: Report feature list, scaling, missingness handling, and protected-attribute treatment.
Evaluate separate curves for demographics, labs, diagnosis sets, and site/time domains before combining.
Check whether low-similarity groups are clinically meaningful and sufficiently powered.
